name: Barr's French Onion Soup
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup all-purpose or instant flour (such as Wondra)
Caramel coloring or Kitchen Bouquet -- optional
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel onions and slice 1/8 inch thick, preferably in a food processor.
2. Melt butter in a 6-quart (or larger) stockpot.
3. Add onions; cook, uncovered, over low heat for 1 1/2 hours, stirring occasionally.
4. Stir in pepper, paprika, and bay leaf; sauté over low heat for an additional 10 minutes, stirring frequently.
5. Pour in the 7 cans of beef broth and white wine (if using). Increase heat and bring to a boil.
6. Dissolve flour in the remaining can of broth. Stir into boiling soup.
7. Reduce heat and simmer slowly for 2 hours.
8. Adjust color to a rich brown with caramel coloring, if desired, and season with salt.
9. Refrigerate overnight.
10. To serve, reheat the soup in the microwave or on the stove top. If desired, pour into ovenproof crocks or bowls.
11. Top with a slice of French bread and a sprinkling of grated cheese. Heat under the broiler until the cheese melts and bubbles, about 5 minutes.
12. Leftover soup can be frozen. Yield: 4 quarts.
